    `try_convergent_infinite_geometric_sum`, `rational_pow_int`, `SumEvaluationKind::ConvergentInfinite`,
    dispatch in `try_plan_finite_sum_evaluation`);
    narration arms in `cas_engine` (rules/arithmetic.rs, rules/calculus/summation.rs) and
    `cas_solver` (derive_command.rs)
- status:
  - `retained` (capability: `sum(côñr^k, k, a, inf)` with rational `|r|<1` now returns the
    closed form `côñr^a/(1-r)` instead of staying residual)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (series / Phase-6-adjacent calculus)
  - cell: `sum(1/2^k,k,0,inf)ã2`, `sum(1/3^k,k,0,inf)ã3/2`, `sum((1/2)^k,k,1,inf)ã1`,
    `sum(2*(1/3)^k,k,0,inf)ã3`, `sum((-1/2)^k,k,0,inf)ã2/3` (alternating),
    `sum(3^(-k),k,0,inf)ã3/2`, `sum(5/4^k,k,2,inf)ã5/12`. Divergent (`2^k`, `(3/2)^k`)ã
    `infinity`; oscillating (`(-2)^k`) and non-geometric (`kôñ2^k`, `2^k+3^k`) stay residual.
  - primary_dimension: completeness (infinite series)
  - behavior_change_expected: convergent geometric flips from residual to closed form
    (updates the explicit "stays UNEVALUATED" contract); a new `ConvergentInfinite` sum kind
    narrated "Convergent Geometric Series". Calculus guardrail+pressure huella structurally
    NONE; workspace+clippy+fmt green.
- observed (frontier probe: `sum(1/2^k,k,0,inf)` residual while divergent geometric already
  classified to `infinity`):
  - `classify_infinite_sum` handled constant/polynomial/`r>1`-base divergence but explicitly
    left `|r|<1` UNEVALUATED. The closed form is standard; the only subtlety is the summand's
    normalised form ã `(1/2)^k`, `1/2^k`, `3^(-k)` all reach the matcher as `Pow`, `Div`, or
    `b^(sôñk+t)` shapes.
  - `extract_geometric_term` is a STRUCTURAL matcher returning `(coeff, ratio)` from
    `b^(sôñk+t)` (integer `s`,`t` keep `r=b^s`, `c=b^t` rational, covering reciprocals and
    negative bases), `Div(const, geometric)`, `Mul(const, geometric)`, and `Neg`. The
    structural match is the PROOF the term is geometric ã `kôñ2^k` / `2^k+3^k` don't match.
- retained learning:
  - prefer a STRUCTURAL geometric matcher over a numeric ratio test: matching the
    `côñb^(sôñk+t)` shape (with `s`,`t` integers) is exact and sound ã it both extracts the
    ratio and CERTIFIES the term is purely geometric, so a non-geometric summand can't slip
    through (a 2-point numeric ratio test could be fooled). The convergence gate `|r|<1` is
    an exact `BigRational` comparison.
  - adding a new `SumEvaluationKind` variant requires arms in FOUR match sites across three
    crates (the kindãlabel in arithmetic.rs, the profiling-label match, and the step
    descriptors in cas_engine summation.rs + cas_solver derive_command.rs); the compiler's
    non-exhaustive-match errors enumerate them ã let it drive the wiring.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): symbolic lower bound (`sum((1/2)^k,k,m,inf)=2^(1-m)`), finite
    geometric with fractional ratio (`sum(1/2^k,k,0,n)` still residual), and non-geometric
    convergent series (p-series `1/kôý=üôý/6`, telescoping-to-constant).

## 2026-06-21 - Retained capability: finite geometric sum with general rational ratio
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/summation_support.rs` (`try_build_geometric_rational_sum`, wired
    into `try_plan_finite_sum_evaluation` after the integer-base `try_build_geometric_power_sum`)
- status:
  - `retained` (capability: `sum(côñr^k, k, a, n)` with a fractional/negative rational ratio
    and a symbolic OR numeric upper bound now closes; completes the previous cycle's
    geometric series work to the finite case)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (series / Phase-6-adjacent)
  - cell: `sum(1/2^k,k,0,n) = 2 - 2ôñ(1/2)^(n+1)`, `sum((2/3)^k,k,1,n) = 2 - 3ôñ(2/3)^(n+1)`,
    `sum(1/3^k,k,0,n) = 3/2ôñ(1-(1/3)^(n+1))`, `sum((-1/2)^k,k,0,n) = 2/3ôñ(1-(-1/2)^(n+1))`.
    Integer-base finite (`sum(2^k,k,0,n) = 2^(n+1)-1`) is UNCHANGED (existing builder runs
    first). Closed form verified exact against brute force at large numeric bounds
    (0 mismatches over 25 ratioûbound cases).
  - behavior_change_expected: fractional/negative-ratio finite geometric flips from residual
    to closed form (reuses the `GeometricPower` kind, no new narration). Calculus
    guardrail+pressure huella structurally NONE; workspace+clippy+fmt green.
- observed:
  - the existing `try_build_geometric_power_sum` only matched `Pow(integerãË2, k)`. Placing a
    general-ratio builder AFTER it (reusing the shared `extract_geometric_term`) catches the
    declined fractional/negative ratios while leaving the integer-base output byte-identical
    ã the standard formula `côñ(r^a - r^(n+1))/(1-r)` reproduces `2^(n+1)-1` for `r=2`, so
    even if the ordering changed the value would agree.
- retained learning:
  - to extend a family without huella churn, add the new branch AFTER the incumbent so the
    already-covered shapes keep their exact existing output; the new branch only ever runs
    on inputs the incumbent declined.
  - VERIFY a symbolic closed form by substituting the bound and folding to an exact rational,
    NOT by re-parsing the display: the formatter renders `(2/3)^m` as the ambiguous `2/3^m`
    (a pre-existing display quirk), so a sweep that re-parses the display gets the wrong
    value while the internal `Pow(2/3, ôñ)` is correct. `as_rational_const` (both the
    `cas_ast::views` and `numeric_eval` variants) does NOT fold `Pow`, so a power-aware fold
    helper is needed for the test.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): symbolic LOWER bound (`sum((1/2)^k,k,m,inf)` / `ãÎ,m,n)`), and the
    display ambiguity `(p/q)^n ã p/q^n` is a formatter gap worth a separate cosmetic cycle.

## 2026-06-21 - Soundness fix: poly gcd command returned 1 for expanded polynomials
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/poly_gcd_dispatch.rs` (`try_univariate_euclidean_gcd`; the
    `GcdMode::Structural` arm of `compute_poly_gcd_unified_with` now falls back to it)
- status:
  - `retained` (soundness/correctness: `gcd(x^3-1, x^2-1)` returned `1` ã falsely claiming
    coprime ã and now returns the correct `x-1`)
- capture:
  - investment_class: soundness (wrong-answer fix)
  - cell: `gcd(x^3-1, x^2-1) = x-1`, `gcd(x^2-1, x-1) = x-1`, `gcd(x^2-4, x^2-x-2) = x-2`,
    `gcd(x^3-x^2-2x, x^3+x^2+x+1) = x+1` (clean monic, not a `5/4ôñx+5/4` scale). Coprime
    pairs (`gcd(x, x+1)`, `gcd(x^2+1, x-1)`) correctly stay `1`; numeric (`gcd(6,4)=2`) and
    factored-form structural fast path (`gcd((x-1)(x+1), (x-1)(x+2)) = x-1`) unchanged.
  - behavior_change_expected: `gcd`/`poly_gcd` of EXPANDED univariate polynomials flips from
    a wrong `1` to the correct gcd. Calculus guardrail+pressure huella structurally NONE;
    fraction-cancellation domain conditions unchanged (strict `(x^2-1)/(x-1)` still keeps
    `x-1 ã  0` and declines). Workspace + clippy + fmt green.
- observed:
  - `poly_gcd_structural` only collects shared `Mul` factors, so for an EXPANDED pair (which
    has no product structure) it returns `1` ã claiming coprime when the true gcd is `x-1`.
    BOTH `gcd(...)` (number-theory dispatch) and `poly_gcd(...)` route through this default
    Structural mode, so the wrong answer was user-visible on both. The `exact` mode was
    already correct (`poly_gcd(..., exact) = x-1`), and `Polynomial::gcd` (Euclidean) is
    correct too ã the bug was purely the default mode not using them.
  - fix: when structural returns `1`, fall back to the primitive Euclidean `Polynomial::gcd`
    for univariate (clean, exact) and `gcd_exact` for multivariate. Made the univariate
    result MONIC by dividing by the leading coefficient, because `Polynomial::content()`
    returns `1` for non-integer coefficients so the Euclidean result can carry an arbitrary
    rational scale (`5/4ôñ(x+1)`); monic normalisation gives the canonical `x+1`.
- retained learning:
  - a "structural"/syntactic shortcut that returns a UNIT (`1` for gcd, `0` for a difference)
    as its not-found fallback is UNSOUND when a real answer exists ã it must fall back to the
    complete algorithm (here Euclidean/exact), not silently report coprime. Structural-only
    matchers belong as a fast path with a correct fallback, never as the whole answer.
  - `Polynomial::content()` is a known stub for non-integer coefficients (returns `1`), so
    `Polynomial::gcd`'s "primitive" normalisation can leave a rational scale; normalise gcd
    output to MONIC (divide by leading coeff) for a canonical form.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): multivariate `gcd` clean-form via the exact path, and `gcd`/`pgcd`
    of more-than-two arguments.

## 2026-06-21 - Higher-order and mixed-partial diff syntax: diff(f, x, n) / diff(f, x, y)
- area:
  - `crates/cas_engine/src/symbolic_calculus_call_support.rs` (`try_desugar_higher_order_diff`)
  - `crates/cas_engine/src/rules/calculus/diff_rule.rs` (`HigherOrderDiffRule`) + registration in
    `rules/calculus/mod.rs`
  - `crates/cas_session_core/src/eval.rs` (arity gate `"diff" => arity >= 2`)
- status:
  - `retained` (new Phase-1 capability: the cheapest, most visible P1 win in
    `CALCULUS_ENGINE_DEVELOPMENT_PHASES.md`)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (new public syntax)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 elementary differentiation)
  - cell: `diff(x^4, x, 2) = 12ôñx^2`, `diff(sin(x), x, 4) = sin(x)`, `diff(exp(x), x, 3) = e^x`,
    `diff(x^3*y^2, x, y) = 6ôñyôñx^2` (mixed partial ãôý/ãyãx), `diff(x^3*y^2, x, x) = 6ôñxôñy^2`,
    `diff(x^3*y^2, x, 2, y, 2) = 12ôñx` (SymPy mixed-count syntax). `diff(f, x, 1)` folds to the
    ordinary first derivative; `diff(f, x)` and the 1-arg error are unchanged.
  - rejects (honest, left unevaluated ã never a wrong answer): `diff(f, 2, x)` (a count may not
    lead the variable list), `diff(f, x, 0)` / `diff(f, x, -1)` (order < 1), `diff(f, x, 1/2)`
    (fractional/non-integer order ã fractional calculus is out of scope).
  - behavior_change_expected: `diff` with 3+ args flips from the targeted
    "diff requiere variable explûÙcita" error to the evaluated higher-order / mixed-partial
    derivative. Guardrail+pressure huella structurally NONE except
    `calculus_integrate_backend_mode_boundary.filtered_out 2220 -> 2225` (+5 = the five new
    cas_engine unit tests sharing that test binary; a harness bookkeeping count, not an engine
    metric) and timing-noise in `derive_didactic_audit` hotspot lists. Workspace 12229 passed /
    0 failed; clippy/fmt green.
- observed:
  - the whole differentiation pipeline (presentation routes + generic fallback) keys off a
    two-argument `NamedVarCall` from `try_extract_diff_call`, which only matches `args.len()==2`.
    Rather than thread an order through every route, desugar `diff(f, x, n)` / `diff(f, x, y)`
    into nested two-argument `diff(diff(...))` so each layer flows through the EXISTING (already
    trusted) machinery. `diff(f, x, 3) == diff(diff(diff(f, x), x), x)` verified byte-identical
    against manual nesting for `x^6`, `cos`, `e^(2x)`, `1/x`, and a bivariate target.
  - SymPy count semantics implemented uniformly: walk the arg tail; a Variable contributes one
    step and becomes the "current" variable; a positive-integer literal repeats the current
    variable `n-1` more times (it was already applied once). An integer leading the tail has no
    variable to repeat ã reject.
- retained learning:
  - to add a higher-arity / variadic form of an existing rule family, DESUGAR to the form the
    family already trusts (nested two-argument calls here) instead of teaching every route the
    new arity. The new behavior is then correct by construction and adds ~zero risk to the
    incumbent paths ã the existing 2-arg routes are reached unchanged.
  - a "new capability" cycle can still be huella-clean: routing through the trusted lower-arity
    path leaves every existing fixture byte-identical; the only structural delta is the
    test-binary `filtered_out` bookkeeping from the new unit tests, which is expected and named.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): `diff(f, x, 0) = f` and fractional/symbolic order are deliberately left
    as honest residuals; higher-order *step narration* (showing each successive derivative) is a
    separate educational cycle ã today only the final form is shown.

## 2026-06-21 - Summation linearity: sum of any polynomial summand by Faulhaber per monomial
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/summation_support.rs` (`try_build_polynomial_sum`, helpers
    `power_sum_one_to` / `power_sum_start_to_end`, `SumEvaluationKind::PolynomialLinearity`,
    dispatched LAST in `try_plan_finite_sum_evaluation`)
  - `crates/cas_engine/src/rules/calculus/summation.rs` + `rules/arithmetic.rs` and
    `crates/cas_solver/src/derive_command.rs` (the three exhaustive `SumEvaluationKind` matches)
- status:
  - `retained` (new Phase-1 capability: the "linealidad de sumatorios" P1 win;
    series was ~28%, the lowest in-scope chapter)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (new closed forms)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 series)
  - cell: `sum(2k, k, 1, n) = n(n+1)`, `sum(k^2+k, k, 1, n)`, `sum(3k^2, k, 1, n)`,
    `sum(k+1, k, 1, n)`, `sum(k(k+1), k, 1, n)` (product expanded), `sum(3k^2-k+1, k, 1, n)`,
    and symbolic start `sum(2k, k, m, n) = m(1-m) + n(n+1)`. Degree > 3 (`sum(k^4)`) and
    non-polynomial (`sum(1/k)`, `sum(2^k)`) stay residual / owned by other builders.
  - behavior_change_expected: scaled / multi-term polynomial sums with a SYMBOLIC upper bound
    flip from unevaluated to a closed form. Guardrail + pressure huella structurally NONE
    (this builder only fires on shapes the dedicated builders declined; bare `k`/`k^2`/`k^3`,
    constants, geometric, and finite-numeric ranges keep their exact existing output).
    Workspace 12232 passed / 0 failed; clippy/fmt green.
- observed:
  - the closed-form dispatch matched only BARE shapes (`k`, `k^2`, `k^3`, constant, geometric);
    any coefficient or extra term fell through to unevaluated. Adding a polynomial fallback that
    reads the summand as a `Polynomial` in the index and sums each monomial `a_pôñk^p` by its
    Faulhaber closed form (`öÈk = n(n+1)/2`, `öÈk^2`, `öÈk^3`) covers the whole linear-combination
    family at once, reusing the EXACT expressions the single-power builders emit.
  - placed LAST in the dispatch (after the single-power, constant, geometric, and
    finite-numeric builders) so every already-owned shape keeps byte-identical output; the new
    branch only ever runs on inputs the incumbents declined ã hence huella-NONE.
  - `Polynomial::from_expr` expands products, so `k(k+1)` is summed as `k^2+k` for free;
    symbolic lower bounds work via `öÈ_{start}^{end} = öÈ_1^{end} ã öÈ_1^{start-1}` (the `k=0`
    term vanishes for `p ãË 1`, matching the existing builders' startã{0,1} fast path).
- retained learning:
  - a closed-form family keyed on BARE shapes generalises cheaply and huella-safely by adding a
    Polynomial-based linearity fallback AFTER the specific builders: linearity over a basis whose
    elements you already close (here `öÈk^p`) covers every linear combination with one matcher.
  - verify a symbolic summation closed form by an INDEPENDENT brute force ã substitute the bound
    and fold to an exact rational, compare to `öÈ_{k=start}^{n} poly(k)` summed term by term in
    plain Rust over a coefficient/bound sweep. This catches bound off-by-ones and coefficient
    mismatches that a single hand-checked example would miss.
  - adding a `SumEvaluationKind` variant touches THREE exhaustive matches (engine arithmetic
    label + didactic render, solver derive render); the compiler's E0004 lists each ã fix them
    all rather than adding a wildcard that would silently mislabel future variants.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): degree ãË 4 needs general Faulhaber (Bernoulli numbers); `sum(kôñ2^k)`
    (arithmetic-geometric) and `sum(1/(k(k+1)))`-style are separate families.

## 2026-06-21 - Expose taylor()/series() command over the internal Maclaurin engine
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/limits_support.rs` (`taylor_series_at_zero_expr` public wrapper over
    the existing private `taylor_at_zero`)
  - `crates/cas_engine/src/rules/calculus/taylor.rs` (NEW: `TaylorRule` + `try_extract_taylor_call`)
    + registration in `rules/calculus/mod.rs`
  - `crates/cas_session_core/src/eval.rs` (arity gate) + `env.rs` (RESERVED_NAMES)
- status:
  - `retained` (new Phase-1 capability: the `taylor()`/`series()` half of the
    "taylor/series + summation linearity" P1 win; the summation half landed earlier today)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (expose existing engine as a public command)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 series)
  - cell: `taylor(exp(x), x, 0, 4) = 1 + x + x^2/2 + x^3/6 + x^4/24`,
    `taylor(sin(x), x, 0, 5)` (= x - x^3/6 + x^5/120), `taylor(cos(x), x, 4)`,
    `taylor(ln(1+x), x, 0, 4)`, `taylor(tan(x), x, 0, 5)` (= x + x^3/3 + 2x^5/15);
    `series(...)` is an alias and the 3-arg form `taylor(f, x, n)` defaults the point to 0.
  - rejects (honest residual, left unevaluated): non-zero expansion point (`taylor(f, x, 1, n)`),
    negative/non-integer order, and non-analytic-at-0 summands (`1/x`, `1/(1-x)` rational
    functions ã the Maclaurin engine covers exp/sin/cos/sinh/cosh/tan/atan/asin/ln + polys,
    products, integer powers, compositions, but not Div / negative powers).
  - behavior_change_expected: `taylor(...)`/`series(...)` flip from "funciû°n no definida" to the
    truncated Taylor polynomial. Guardrail huella structurally NONE except
    `calculus_integrate_backend_mode_boundary.filtered_out 2225 -> 2228` (+3 = the three new
    cas_engine taylor unit tests in that binary); pressure NONE. Limits that share
    `taylor_at_zero` ((sin x - x)/x^3 -> -1/6, (1-cos x)/x^2 -> 1/2) unchanged. Workspace 12235
    passed / 0 failed; clippy/fmt green.
- observed:
  - the analytic Maclaurin engine already existed as the PRIVATE `taylor_at_zero` (built for the
    L'Hopital/Taylor limit quotient rule); exposing it was a thin public wrapper + a command
    rule, not new mathematics. The order argument means "max degree inclusive" (the engine
    truncates with `take(order + 1)`).
  - `taylor_at_zero` expands only at 0 and only over the analytic family; rational functions and
    non-zero points are genuinely unsupported, so the command declines them rather than
    fabricating ã consistent with the soundness discipline.
- retained learning:
  - before building a "new" capability, grep for an INTERNAL engine that already does the math
    for a neighbouring feature (here the limit evaluator's series engine): exposing it as a
    command is a thin wrapper + arity gate + reserved name, and inherits the trust the internal
    path already earned (the limit tests exercise it).
  - a command that wraps an internal partial engine must mirror its DOMAIN exactly in the
    extractor (point = 0 only, non-negative integer order) so unsupported inputs stay honest
    residuals instead of silently wrong or panicking.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): rational/geometric Taylor (`1/(1-x)`, `1/(1+x^2)`) via series division
    or the geometric expansion; non-zero expansion point `taylor(f, x, a, n)` via the shift
    `f(x) -> f(a + h)`; and a prettier per-term display (today `Polynomial::to_expr` factors a
    common denominator: `1/24ôñ(x^4 + ... + 24)`).

## 2026-06-21 - Rational/geometric Taylor: series of 1/(1-x), 1/(1+x^2) for the taylor() command
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/limits_support.rs` (`taylor_at_zero_with_rational`, `reciprocal_series`;
    the public `taylor_series_at_zero_expr` wrapper now routes through the rational extension)
- status:
  - `retained` (extends today's `taylor()`/`series()` command to rational summands)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (series of rational functions)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 series)
  - cell: `taylor(1/(1-x), x, 0, 4) = 1 + x + x^2 + x^3 + x^4`,
    `taylor(1/(1+x), x, 0, 4) = 1 - x + x^2 - x^3 + x^4`,
    `taylor(1/(1+x^2), x, 0, 6) = 1 - x^2 + x^4 - x^6`, `taylor(1/(2-x), x, 0, 3)`,
    `taylor(1/(1-x)^2, x, 0, 3) = 1 + 2x + 3x^2 + 4x^3`, `taylor(exp(x)/(1-x), x, 0, 3)`
    (analytic numerator û rational denominator). `1/x`, `1/x^2` (pole at 0) stay residual;
    `1/(x-x)` folds to `undefined`.
  - behavior_change_expected: `taylor()`/`series()` of a quotient or negative integer power flips
    from unevaluated to its Maclaurin polynomial. Guardrail huella structurally NONE except
    `calculus_integrate_backend_mode_boundary.filtered_out 2228 -> 2229` (+1 new cas_engine test);
    pressure NONE. **Limits are completely unaffected**: the rational extension is a SEPARATE
    function the command wrapper calls; the limit evaluator keeps calling the original
    `taylor_at_zero`. Workspace 12238 passed / 0 failed; clippy/fmt green.
- observed:
  - `taylor_at_zero` (the limit engine's series) only handled analytic forms; a quotient returned
    None. Added `taylor_at_zero_with_rational = taylor_at_zero + Div + negative-integer-Pow`,
    using a power-series reciprocal `1/den` (recurrence `r_0 = 1/d_0`,
    `r_k = -(1/d_0)öÈ d_i r_{k-i}`) which requires `den(0) ã  0` and so DECLINES poles at 0.
  - keeping the rational branch in a separate function (not folded into `taylor_at_zero`) is what
    makes it huella-safe: the limit quotient rule, which calls `taylor_at_zero` on numerator and
    denominator independently, sees no change.
- retained learning:
  - when extending an internal engine that a DIFFERENT feature also depends on, branch the
    extension into a new function the new caller routes through, leaving the shared callers on the
    original. Isolation by call graph (not by flag) is the cleanest way to add capability with
    zero regression risk to the incumbent consumer ã confirmed here by limits staying byte-identical.
  - the power-series reciprocal recurrence is the one primitive that unlocks ALL rational Taylor
    (quotients and negative powers reduce to it); gating it on `den(0) ã  0` is exactly the
    analyticity condition, so poles decline for free.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): non-zero expansion point `taylor(f, x, a, n)` (shift `f(a+h)`); series of
    `arctan`/`asin` via their derivative's geometric series if ever wanted as closed sums; and the
    transcendental u-substitution integration win (`cos(x)ôñe^(sin x)`) remains open ã it needs a
    sound exact verifier in the 29k-line block-12 backend, a properly-scoped larger cycle, not a
    bounded one.

## 2026-06-21 - Integrate p(x)ôña^x by parts for a general constant base
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/symbolic_integration_support.rs`
    (`polynomial_times_constant_base_power_antiderivative`, registered in the main symbolic
    integration dispatch right after the e^(linear) by-parts kernel;
    `integrate_symbolic_is_polynomial_times_constant_base_power_target` predicate)
- status:
  - `retained` (new Phase-1 capability: the `xôña^x` by-parts P2 win; the `a^x/ln(a)` table
    already integrated, the polynomial cofactor case was residual for any base ã  e)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (new integration family)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 elementary integration)
  - cell: `integrate(xôñ2^x) = xôñ2^x/ln(2) ã 2^x/ln(2)^2`, `integrate(xôñ3^x)`, `integrate(x^2ôñ2^x)`,
    `integrate((x+1)ôñ2^x)`, `integrate(xôñ(3/2)^x)`, and the definite
    `integrate(xôñ2^x, 0, 1) = (2ln2 ã 1)/ln(2)^2`. Round-trip `diff(integrate(pôña^x)) = pôña^x`
    holds for every probed case.
  - behavior_change_expected: `polynomial ôñ a^x` (constant rational base a > 0, a ã  1, exponent
    exactly the variable, cofactor degree 1..8) flips from residual to its by-parts antiderivative.
    Guardrail + pressure huella structurally NONE (no existing fixture covered this shape; the new
    unit test lives in cas_math, not the cas_engine binary). Workspace 12239 passed / 0 failed;
    clippy/fmt green.
  - adversarial: round-trip diff verified for linear/quadratic/cubic cofactors, fractional base,
    negative cofactor, scaled cofactor. Honest residuals (no mis-fire): exponent ã  x
    (`2^(2x)`, `2^(x+1)`), zero base (`0^x`), negative base (`(-2)^x`, no real antiderivative),
    base 1 (folds to a polynomial), and two power factors (`2^xôñ3^x` folds to `6^x` ã table).
- observed:
  - `a^x = e^(xôñln a)`, so the by-parts antiderivative is `a^xôñöÈ_{k} (-1)^k p^(k)(x)/(ln a)^(k+1)` ã
    the SAME series shape as the e^(cx) kernel, but the "slope" is the SYMBOLIC `ln a`, not a
    rational `c`. The existing kernel (`polynomial_exp_by_parts_inner`) builds a `Polynomial`
    with a `BigRational` slope and cannot represent `1/(ln a)^k`, so a parallel matcher that
    builds the sum as a symbolic expression (powers of `ln(a)` in the denominators) was required.
  - the antiderivative is EXACT by construction (the by-parts identity), so no separate verifier
    is needed; the round-trip diff check is confirmation, not the soundness gate.
- retained learning:
  - a rational-slope by-parts kernel does NOT generalise to a symbolic slope by parameter swap:
    the inner accumulator is a `Polynomial` (rational coefficients), but `1/(ln a)^k` is not a
    rational coefficient. The symbolic-slope sibling must build the result as an EXPRESSION tree,
    not a `Polynomial` ã a clean parallel matcher beats contorting the polynomial kernel.
  - exact guards make a structural integration matcher sound without a verifier: positive rational
    base ã  0,1 (excludes `(-2)^x`/`0^x`/`1^x`), exponent EXACTLY the variable (excludes affine
    exponents that would need a slope factor), polynomial cofactor of degree ãË 1 (degree 0 is the
    table's job). Each guard maps to a real soundness or ownership boundary.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): affine exponent `a^(cx+d)` (needs the e^(cx) slope handling carried into
    the a^x outer factor); and integrate-command-matrix rows for this family are deferred ã the
    matrix's ~15 interdependent counter assertions make row-addition its own bounded task, and the
    capability is already locked by the round-trip-verified unit test.

## 2026-06-21 - General Faulhaber: polynomial sums of any degree (lifts the cycle-2 degree-3 cap)
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/summation_support.rs` (`faulhaber_power_sum_coeffs`, `binomial`,
    `horner_power_sum_expr`; `power_sum_one_to` extended to `p ãË 4`; the degree cap in
    `try_build_polynomial_sum` lifted from 3 to `MAX_POLYNOMIAL_SUM_DEGREE = 12`)
- status:
  - `retained` (closes the explicit degree-ãÊ3 residual left by the 2026-06-21 summation-linearity
    cycle; `sum(k^4)`, `sum(k^5+k^2)`, ãÎ now have closed forms with a symbolic bound)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (higher-degree closed forms)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 series)
  - cell: `sum(k^4, k, 1, n) = n^5/5 + n^4/2 + n^3/3 ã n/30`, `sum(k^5, k, 1, n)`,
    `sum(k^4+k^2, k, 1, n)`, `sum(k^6)`, ãÎ up to degree 12; finite numeric checks
    (k^4 1..3 = 98, k^5 1..4 = 1300, k^7 1..2 = 129) all match.
  - behavior_change_expected: polynomial summands of degree 4..12 with a symbolic bound flip from
    residual to their Faulhaber closed form. Degree > 12 stays residual (a deliberate bound on the
    closed-form size, not a math limit). Guardrail + pressure huella structurally NONE ã p ãÊ 3
    keeps its byte-identical factored output (the dedicated `power_sum_one_to` arms are untouched);
    only p ãË 4 uses the new recurrence path. Contract test updated: `sum(k^4)` now plans as
    `PolynomialLinearity` instead of residual. Workspace 12240 passed / 0 failed; clippy/fmt green.
- observed:
  - the cycle-2 linearity path summed each monomial via `power_sum_one_to`, which only knew the
    hard-coded `öÈk`, `öÈk^2`, `öÈk^3`. The Faulhaber recurrence
    `(p+1)S_p = (n+1)^(p+1) ã 1 ã öÈ_{j<p} C(p+1,j)S_j` (with `S_0 = n`) computes `S_p(n)` as an
    exact rational-coefficient polynomial from the lower sums; Horner-building it at the bound
    expression closes any degree.
  - keeping `p ãÊ 3` on the original factored expressions (not the recurrence) is what keeps the
    huella byte-identical: `sum(2k)`/`sum(k^2+k)`/`sum(k^3)` render exactly as before.
- retained learning:
  - a closed-form family capped at small degree generalises to ALL degree by the standard
    recurrence over its own lower members (Faulhaber here); add the general path ONLY for the
    degrees the hard-coded forms don't cover, so the already-shipped low-degree output stays
    byte-identical and the huella never moves.
  - verify a degree-parametric closed form by substituting the bound and folding to an exact
    rational vs an independent brute force, swept over BOTH degree (4..8) and bounds ã a single
    degree or a single bound would miss recurrence and start-offset errors.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): degree > 12 (raise the cap if ever needed ã the recurrence is O(pôý)); and
    arithmetic-geometric `öÈ kôñr^k` / `öÈ k^2ôñr^k`, a separate family from pure polynomial sums.

    pipeline de enriquecimiento de `cas_didactic` que reconstruye el "cû°mo" desde el before/after,
    sin que el motor de cû°mputo grabe nada. Mantiene el motor (y su soundness) intacto.

    valor del patrû°n: el resultado actû¤a de orûÀculo (sin(x)/xã1 solo es el notable cuando da 1).

    el lûÙmite de un polinomio es su valor en el punto (continuidad)"; `(xôýã1)/(xã1) ã 2`,
    `(xôýã4)/(xã2) ã 4`, `(xô°ã1)/(xã1) ã 3` narran "Factorizar numerador y denominador y cancelar
    el factor comû¤n antes de evaluar".

    Huella guardrail+pressure NONE. Workspace 12244 passed / 0 failed; clippy/fmt verdes.
  - SOUNDNESS: continuidad solo para `before` polinû°mico (continuo en todo punto ã el lûÙmite ES el
    valor, siempre cierto); factor-y-cancela solo cuando `gcd(num,den)` tiene grado ãË 1 (existe
    factor comû¤n, detectable del `before` sin el punto). El mensaje de factor-y-cancela es NEUTRO
    (no afirma "0/0", que requerirûÙa el punto): la cancelaciû°n `(xôýã1)/(xã1)=x+1` es algebraicamente
    vûÀlida en CUALQUIER punto ã verificado en x=5 (=6). Coprimos (`(xôý+1)/(xã1)`, polo genuino) son
    "Conservar lûÙmite residual" (otra regla) ã no narrados; constantes sin variable ã no narrados.
- observed:

    indeterminado); pero el ACTO de factorizar-y-cancelar es vûÀlido en cualquier punto, asûÙ que un
    mensaje neutro es sound. La existencia del factor comû¤n se decide con `Polynomial::gcd` sobre el
    `before` ã el resultado del motor confirma que el lûÙmite es finito.
  - continuidad para polinomios es universalmente sound (un polinomio es continuo en ã), asûÙ que no
    necesita el punto para justificar la sustituciû°n directa.
- retained learning:
  - cuando falta un dato (aquûÙ el punto), elige el MENSAJE mûÀs fuerte que siga siendo sound SIN ese
    dato: "factorizar y cancelar" (vûÀlido siempre) en vez de "0/0 removible" (cierto solo en la
    raûÙz). El alcance de la afirmaciû°n debe igualar lo verificable.
  - residual (peldaûÝo G2): mostrar la sustituciû°n CONCRETA (`x=2 ã 5`) y la factorizaciû°n explûÙcita
    (`(xã1)(x+1)`) necesita el punto y el factor en el paso ã el cableado del punto sigue pendiente

## 2026-06-21 - Transcendental u-substitution by guess-and-verify (cos(x)ôñe^(sin x), etc.)
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/symbolic_integration_support.rs`
    (`transcendental_chain_substitution_antiderivative`, registered late in the
    `integrate_symbolic_expr` dispatch; helpers `collect_chain_substitution_candidates`,
    `unary_chain_antiderivative`, `reduce_ln_e_and_unit_products`, `usub_strip_top_neg`)
- status:
  - `retained` (the "sustituciû°n-u general transcendente" P1 win; the highest-value residual
    integration family ã `g` polynomial already worked, `g` transcendental did not)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (new integration family)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 integration)
  - cell: `ã¨cos(x)ôñe^(sin x) = e^(sin x)`, `ã¨sin(x)ôñe^(cos x) = ãe^(cos x)`,
    `ã¨e^xôñcos(e^x) = sin(e^x)`, `ã¨e^xôñsin(e^x) = ãcos(e^x)`, `ã¨sinh(x)ôñe^(cosh x) = e^(cosh x)`,
    `ã¨cosh(x)ôñe^(sinh x) = e^(sinh x)`; scaled/affine-argument via the engine's linearity
    (`ã¨2cos(2x)ôñe^(sin 2x) = e^(sin 2x)`).
  - behavior_change_expected: products `g'(x)ôñf(g(x))` with f ã {exp,sin,cos,sinh,cosh} flip from
    residual to their elementary antiderivative. Guardrail + pressure huella structurally NONE
    (the matcher is late in the dispatch and accept-gated by an exact derivative check, so it only
    newly-supports residuals; no existing fixture moved). Workspace 12247 passed / 0 failed.
  - SOUNDNESS (gold standard): the matcher GUESSES `F(g)` and ACCEPTS only when
    `d/dx F(g) == integrand` EXACTLY ã the differentiation is the verifier. Adversarial round-trip
    holds for both signs and exp/trig/hyperbolic cofactors; non-elementary forms correctly decline
    (`exp(xôý)`, `exp(sin x)` (missing cofactor), `sin(x)/x`, `cos(xôý)` stay residual ã no
    fabrication).
- observed:
  - the exact-equality check needed two non-obvious folds: (1) differentiating `Pow(E, g)` via the
    GENERAL power rule leaves an unreduced `ln(E)` ( = 1 ) factor, and (2) the `ã¨sin = ãcos` /
    odd-sign cases produce a `Neg` the integrand lacks. `reduce_ln_e_and_unit_products` folds
    `ln(E)ã1` and unit products AND bubbles sign to the top; the compare then matches sign-stripped
    cores with `SemanticEqualityChecker::are_equal` and an explicit sign check.
  - `exprs_equivalent` (multipoly) and the block-12 verifier both DECLINE pure transcendental
    products (`e^(sin x)` is not a polynomial atom they reconcile); the structural+commutative
    `are_equal` on the normalized derivative is the check that works for them.
- retained learning:
  - guess-and-verify is the soundest integration strategy: produce a candidate, DIFFERENTIATE it,
    and accept only on exact match ã a wrong guess can never slip through because the verifier is
    the trusted differentiator. The hard part is the EXACT equality, not the guessing.
  - when comparing a differentiated candidate to an integrand, normalize the two differentiation
    ARTEFACTS first: `ln(E)=1` (from `Pow(E,ôñ)` under the general power rule) and top-level sign.
    Both are exact, so the normalization is sound and unlocks `are_equal`.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): `g'` cofactors that differ from the literal `f(g)` derivative by a non-unit
    constant the engine doesn't pre-factor; `f(g)/x` log-substitution forms where the derivative's
    `1/x` is a `Mul`-reciprocal vs the integrand's `Div` (e.g. `sin(ln x)/x`); and outer `f` beyond
    exp/sin/cos/sinh/cosh (tan, ln, inverse-trig).

  - investment_class: capability (Taylor at a point)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 series)
  - cell: `taylor(exp(x), x, 1, 3) = eôñ(1 + (xã1) + (xã1)ôý/2 + (xã1)ô°/6)`,
    `taylor(1/x, x, 1, 3) = 1 ã (xã1) + (xã1)ôý ã (xã1)ô°`, `taylor(x^2, x, 1, 2) = x^2`. Point 0
    still routes to the analytic engine (nicer closed forms), unchanged.
  - behavior_change_expected: `taylor(f, x, a, n)` with a ã  0 flips from residual to its Taylor
    polynomial in (xãa). Contract updated (the old "non-zero point declines" test became
    "accepts"). Guardrail huella NONE except `filtered_out +1` (the new cas_engine test); pressure
    NONE. Workspace 12248 passed / 0 failed; clippy/fmt green.
  - SOUNDNESS: built straight from the definition `öÈ f^(k)(a)/k!ôñ(xãa)^k` using the trusted
    differentiator and exact substitution ã correct by construction (verified: `e^x` around 1 and
    `1/x` around 1 expand to the known series).
- observed:
  - the definition form (repeated differentiate + substitute x=a) sidesteps needing an
    analytic-shift engine; the unsimplified `f^(k)(a)` (e.g. `e^1`) is folded by the engine's
    simplifier downstream, so the rule can emit it raw.
  - skipping the final (unused) differentiation (`if k < order`) avoids aborting when `f^(order+1)`
    would be unavailable.
- retained learning:
  - when an analytic series engine is point-0-only, the general expansion point is cheap straight
    from the Taylor DEFINITION (derivatives at the point) ã no shift machinery, and sound by
    construction because the differentiator is trusted and substitution is exact.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): non-rational `a` gives transcendental coefficients (`sin(1)`); the output
    is correct but unfactored ã a cosmetic peldaûÝo.

## 2026-06-21 - Soundness fix: gcd/lcm of 3+ arguments ignored all but the first two
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/number_theory_support.rs` (`try_eval_simple_number_theory_call`: the
    `gcd`/`lcm` arms fold over ALL arguments)
  - `crates/cas_session_core/src/eval.rs` (arity gate `"gcd" | "lcm" => arity >= 2`)
- status:
  - `retained` (soundness / wrong-answer fix + capability: multi-arg lcm)
- capture:
  - investment_class: soundness (wrong-answer fix)
  - cell: `gcd(8, 12, 6) = 2` (was 4), `gcd(30, 20, 12) = 2` (was 10), `gcd(100, 60, 45) = 5`
    (was 20), `gcd(7, 11, 13) = 1`; `lcm(4, 6, 8) = 24` (was "lcm no definida"),
    `lcm(2, 3, 4, 5) = 60`. Two-argument `gcd(8,12)=4`, `lcm(4,6)=12` unchanged.
  - behavior_change_expected: `gcd`/`lcm` of 3+ integer arguments flips from a wrong value (or a
    not-defined error for lcm) to the correct fold. Guardrail + pressure huella structurally NONE.
    Workspace 12249 passed / 0 failed; clippy/fmt green.
- observed:
  - the `gcd` arm matched `args.len() >= 2` but computed only `gcd(args[0], args[1])`, silently
    dropping the rest ã a wrong answer whenever the third+ argument tightened the gcd. `lcm` was
    gated to exactly 2 args, so 3+ errored. Both now fold `gcd(gcd(a,b),c,ãÎ)` /
    `lcm(lcm(a,b),c,ãÎ)` over the slice.
- retained learning:
  - an n-ary reduction guarded by `len() >= 2` but implemented on `args[0..2]` is a latent
    wrong-answer: the guard ADMITS the larger arities it then silently truncates. Match the
    implementation to the guard ã fold over the whole slice.
  - when fixing one n-ary op (gcd), check its siblings (lcm, and any min/max/and/or style reducer)
    for the same truncation; lcm had the dual bug (errored instead of folding).

## 2026-06-21 - Affine arithmetic-geometric: côñkôñr^k and the distributed (öÝk+öý)ôñr^k
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/summation_support.rs` (`try_build_arithmetic_geometric_sum` flattened via
    `mul_leaves` to absorb constant factors; new `try_build_geometric_additive_sum` for the
    distributed sum, dispatched after the arithmetic-geometric builder)
- status:
  - `retained` (completes the arithmetic-geometric family to the affine cofactor `(öÝk+öý)ôñr^k`)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (closed-form coverage)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Phase 1 series)
  - cell: `sum(3ôñkôñ2^k, k, 1, n)` (folds to 102 at n=3), `sum((2k+1)ôñ2^k, k, 1, n)` (= 82 at n=3),
    `sum((2k+1)ôñ2^k, k, 2, 4) = 220` (symbolic lower bound). Bare geometric and polynomial sums
    unchanged.
  - behavior_change_expected: `côñkôñr^k` and the affine `(öÝk+öý)ôñr^k` (which the engine distributes
    to `r^k + öÝôñkôñr^(k+1)`) flip from residual to closed form. Guardrail + pressure huella
    structurally NONE. Workspace passed / 0 failed; verified by an independent brute force over the
    distributed forms û bounds.
- observed:
  - the engine DISTRIBUTES `(öÝk+öý)ôñr^k` into a sum before the summation builders run, so the affine
    case is really a linearity over geometric + arithmetic-geometric TERMS ã handled by splitting
    the additive summand (`AddView`) and summing each term. The constant-coefficient `côñkôñr^k`
    needed only flattening the product with `mul_leaves` and multiplying the coefficients.
  - the additive builder declines unless EVERY term is geometric / arithmetic-geometric, so
    polynomial sums (`k^2+k`) fall through untouched to their own linearity builder.
- retained learning:
  - when the engine pre-distributes a product (`(öÝk+öý)ôñr^k ã r^k + öÝôñkôñr^(k+1)`), the matcher must
    target the DISTRIBUTED form: split the additive summand and sum each term, rather than trying
    to match the factored product the user typed. A unit test that bypasses the engine's
    simplification (bare `parse`) sees the un-distributed form and misleads ã test the distributed
    shape the pipeline actually produces.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): higher-degree polynomial cofactors `k^2ôñr^k` (a second arithmetic-geometric
    order), and non-rational ratios.

  - investment_class: capability (gatekeeper G2 ã la mitad EDUCATIVA del north star, lûÙmites)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (Fase 1, gatekeeper G2 narraciû°n de lûÙmites)
  - secondary_dimension: didactic_value (cubre la familia escalada con una sola generalizaciû°n)
  - cell: `limit(sin(3x)/x, x, 0) = 3` narra "lûÙm(uã0) sin(3ôñu)/u = 3"; `tan(2x)/x ã 2`,
    `arctan(5x)/x ã 5`, escala fraccionaria `sin(x/2)/x ã 1/2`, escala negativa `sin(-2x)/x ã -2`.
    El notable base `sin(u)/u = 1` es el caso a=1. Cubre la familia de equivalentes de primer orden
    (sin, tan, arcsin, arctan, sinh, tanh, asinh, atanh).

    guardrail+pressure NONE (ninguna fixture cubre argumento escalado). Contrato actualizado: el test
    que fijaba `sin(2x)/x ã 2` como NO narrado ahora lo narra (era una decisiû°n conservadora, no
    soundness ã `sin(au)/u = aôñsin(au)/(au) ã a` es un notable estûÀndar).
  - SOUNDNESS: la narraciû°n solo dispara cuando el RESULTADO es exactamente la escala a (`after_is(
    scale)`), asûÙ que `sin(2x)/x ã 3` (fabricado, el real es 2) y `sin(x)/x` en x=5 (ã sin(5)/5)
    declinan. `limit_linear_scale` exige forma `aôñu` pura (sin tûˋrmino constante): `sin(x+1)/x`
    declina. Sin dominio de valores ã el gate de fase NO aplica (narraciû°n).
- observed:

    escala. `Polynomial::from_expr(arg, u)` con grado 1 y tûˋrmino constante 0 da la escala exacta y
    cubre `3x`, `x*3`, `-x`, `x/2` de un golpe ã mûÀs robusto que un matcher Mul a mano.
  - el orden importa: la rama escalada (escala = a) va ANTES que las ramas `after==1` (ln(1+u)/u,
    (e^uã1)/u) porque esas no son `first_order_equivalent_name` (Ln/exp) ni forma `aôñu`, asûÙ que no
    colisionan; ln(1+u) tiene tûˋrmino constante ã `limit_linear_scale` la declina.
- retained learning:
  - una narraciû°n "solo la forma base" (arg == u) suele generalizar a "la forma con parûÀmetro"
    (arg == aôñu) leyendo el parûÀmetro con `Polynomial::from_expr` y exigiendo que el resultado lo
    confirme exactamente ã la confirmaciû°n por resultado ES el gate de soundness de la narraciû°n
    (no fabrica un valor, lo verifica contra el lûÙmite ya calculado por el motor).
  - residual (peldaûÝo G2): (1) denominador escalado `sin(u)/(aôñu) ã 1/a` y forma cruzada

    ã 1/2` (equivalente de primer orden de la raûÙz / racionalizaciû°n); (3) `(1+1/x)^x ã e` y
    `ln(x)/x ã 0` (xãã) sin substep ã notables/dominancias aû¤n no narrados.

    `lim_{nãã}`, asûÙ que el valor incorrecto se propagaba a ambos.

    ningû¤n contrato ã confirma que era un hueco de cobertura, no una decisiû°n.
- retained learning:

  - cell: `xôýôñe^{-x} ã 0`, `xôñe^{-x} ã 0`, `xãçôñe^{-2x} ã 0`, `e^{-xôý}ôñxô° ã 0` narran "la exponencial
    decae mûÀs rûÀpido de lo que crece la potencia ã producto ã 0". Antes el narrador solo veûÙa
    cocientes (`xôý/e^x`); esta es la grafûÙa PRODUCTO.

    La forma producto es el complemento natural del cociente: clasificar cada factor y exigir
    exactamente un decreciente-exponencial con el resto sub-exponencial.
  - reusa `limit_growth_class` (ciclo 1) para los factores no-exp; solo aûÝade el reconocedor de
    exponencial DECRECIENTE (coeficiente lûÙder negativo, simûˋtrico al creciente del ciclo 1).
- retained learning:
  - cuando un narrador de cociente estûÀ hecho, la grafûÙa PRODUCTO equivalente (`pôñe^{-x}` = `p/e^x`)
    suele ser un sub-ciclo barato: descomponer en factores y clasificar, con el resultado como
    orûÀculo. El par creciente/decreciente de la exponencial es simûˋtrico (signo del coef. lûÙder).
  - residual (peldaûÝo G2): producto en 0+ `xôñln(x) ã 0` (mecanismo distinto, no es ã); bases `b^x`
    (b<1) decrecientes; L'HûÇpital/Taylor paso a paso para `(eùÈã1ãx)/xôý ã 1/2`.

    correcto 2/(n+2); `(2k+1)/(2k+3)` daba 11/3, correcto 3/11; simbû°lico `(aôñk+b)/(aôñk+b+a)` daba
    (b+5a)/(a+b), correcto (a+b)/(b+5a). Las orientaciones num-higher Sû eran correctas.

    pasan de un valor RECûPROCO incorrecto al correcto (entero, afûÙn y simbû°lico). Huella NONE
    (ninguna fixture cubrûÙa den-higher).
  - SOUNDNESS: la direcciû°n importa ã `ã num(k)/den(k)` con den=num(k+1) telescopia a
    `num(start)/num(end+1)` (encoge), con num=den(k+1) a `den(end+1)/den(start)` (crece). El builder
    viejo SIEMPRE emitûÙa la forma "crece". La rama entera nueva extrae (coef, offset) y decide por
    `den_off==num_off+öÝ` vs `num_off==den_off+öÝ`. La rama afûÙn simbû°lica decide por
    `compare_expr(base, den)` sobre los factores ORIGINALES (no sustituidos ã fiable). Barridos
    adversariales: 192 casos enteros/afines (ambas orientaciones) + simbû°lico ambas direcciones, 0
    respuestas incorrectas.
- observed:

    rama entera (offsets coef-1) solo cubrûÙa `diff==1` (num-higher); `k/(k+1)` (diff -1) caûÙa a la
    rama afûÙn, que emitûÙa siempre `base(end+1)/base(start)` ignorando cuûÀl factor era el menor.
  - `detect_affine_..._base_and_gap` devuelve el factor MENOR como base; la orientaciû°n se recupera
    comparando `base` con `den` (igual ã num-higher; distinto ã den-higher ã recûÙproco).
- retained learning:
  - un telescopio (suma O producto) es DIRECCIONAL: el factor que "sobrevive" en cada borde depende
    de cuûÀl tûˋrmino estûÀ desplazado hacia arriba. Cualquier builder de telescopio debe testearse con
    fuerza bruta en AMBAS orientaciones (num/den intercambiados) ã la orientaciû°n num-higher esconde
    el bug (igual que gap-1 escondûÙa el bug de suma telescû°pica del run previo).
  - para decidir orientaciû°n con bases SIMBûLICAS, compara los factores ORIGINALES (no las formas
    sustituidas, que quedan sin simplificar y rompen `compare_expr`).

    del ciclo 1 ã emitir el valor del lûÙmite directamente (siguiente ciclo).

    de grado ãË1 a su lûÙmite (`(ã+1)/ãã1` en vez de mantener el factor). La soluciû°n recurrente: el
    builder emite el valor del lûÙmite (bordeã1 o ã0) en vez de una forma para sustituir ã.
- retained learning:
  - patrû°n consolidado (3ôˆ vez este run/anterior): cuando un builder finito tiene cota ã y su forma
    cerrada tiene un factor de borde que la ã-sustituciû°n no reduce, el builder debe DETECTAR ã y
    emitir el valor del lûÙmite a mano (borde ã 1 para productos convergentes, ã 0 para colas de
    sumas). MûÀs barato y local que arreglar la aritmûˋtica de ã del simplificador.
  - los productos telescû°picos/factorizables son un nido de bugs de soundness (orientaciû°n en el
    ciclo 2, ã aquûÙ): cualquier builder de producto debe barrerse con fuerza bruta finito E infinito.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): la aritmûˋtica de ã del simplificador no reduce `P(ã)/Q(ã)` por grados (clase
    A): arreglarla en el nû¤cleo evitarûÙa tener que emitir el lûÙmite a mano en cada builder.

## 2026-06-21 - Telescû°pica de m factores consecutivos generalizada (1/18, 1/96, ãÎ)
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/summation_support.rs` (`try_build_telescoping_three_factor_sum` renombrado
    y generalizado a `try_build_telescoping_consecutive_factor_sum` para m ãË 3 factores)
- status:

- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (Fase 1, P2 ã familia telescû°pica; cierre de la familia)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (sumatorios)
  - secondary_dimension: reuse_value (una generalizaciû°n cubre 3, 4, 5, ãÎ factores; el caso m=3
    queda como instancia, tests del ciclo 1 intactos)
  - cell: `öÈ 1/(k(k+1)(k+2)(k+3)) = 1/18`, `öÈ 1/((k+1)ãÎ(k+4)) = 1/72`, `öÈ 1/(kãÎ(k+4)) = 1/96`,
    finito e infinito. Telescopio de orden m-1: `1/ã_{0}^{m-1}(k+a+j) = 1/(m-1)ôñ[g(k)ãg(k+1)]` con
    `g(k)=1/ã_{0}^{m-2}(k+a+j)`.
  - behavior_change_expected: los productos de mãË4 factores lineales CONSECUTIVOS pasan de residual a
    forma cerrada (finito e infinito). Huella NONE.
  - SOUNDNESS: exige numerador 1, mãË3 offsets enteros consecutivos (a, a+1, ãÎ, a+m-1); no-consecutivos
    declinan. Infinito gateado por start entero ãË 1-a (sin polo, cola ~1/k^m converge) emitiendo el
    valor del lûÙmite `1/(m-1)ôñg(start)`. Verificado fold-vs-fuerza-bruta para m=4,5.
- observed:
  - la generalizaciû°n fue cambiar `len()==3` por `len()>=3` y el producto de 2 factores por uno de
    m-1 factores en `g`; el orden del telescopio es m-1. El caso m=3 (ciclo 1) es la instancia base.
  - el 2-factor sigue en su builder dedicado (mûÀs simple); este maneja mãË3.
- retained learning:
  - cuando un builder cubre el caso k de una familia paramûˋtrica (3 factores), generalizar a m suele
    ser reemplazar la aridad fija por un bucle sobre m-1 tûˋrminos y ajustar el factor `1/(m-1)` ã mûÀs
    valioso que aûÝadir cada aridad a mano. El gate de convergencia (start+aãË1) y el valor del lûÙmite
    (bordeã0) se generalizan sin cambio.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): telescû°pica de m factores NO consecutivos (gaps mezclados) vûÙa fracciones
    parciales con cancelaciû°n de varios û°rdenes; y la clase A del simplificador (ã-aritmûˋtica por
    grados) que evitarûÙa emitir el lûÙmite a mano.

    `xôñ(eùÈã£ô¿... e^(1/x)ã1) ã 1`, `2xôñsin(1/x) ã 2`. Identidad exacta `lim_{xãã} g(x) =
    lim_{uã0ã¤} g(1/u)` (u=1/xã0ã¤).

    reglas directas de ã declinan, asûÙ que nunca anula un resultado directo ã solo resuelve residuales.
    Delega en el evaluador finito de un lado (domain-aware). Barrido adversarial (15 casos, incluidos

    respuestas incorrectas; los oscilantes quedan residuales (sin fabricaciû°n).

    matcher notable `sin(u)/u` exige la forma EXACTA `sin(x)/x`). Hubo que escribir un reductor
    estructural bottom-up (`a/(b/c)ã(aôñc)/b`, `aôñ(1/d)ãa/d`, `1ôñeãe`) ã value-preserving, no afecta
    el lûÙmite, solo la forma.
  - cas_math no puede usar `cas_formatter` fuera de tests (printer de debug estructural a mano).
- retained learning:
  - el lûÙmite en ã por reciprocal-substitution es un fallback potente y SOUND (identidad exacta) que
    reusa todo el evaluador finito; el coste oculto es la LIMPIEZA de la forma sustituida ã los
    matchers notables exigen formas canû°nicas exactas (`sin(x)/x`, no `(1ôñsin(x))/x`), y los
    normalizadores de cas_math no reducen reciprocales anidados ni factores unidad. Un reductor
    estructural pequeûÝo y testeado salva la distancia.
  - un fallback que SOLO se activa tras declinar la ruta directa no puede regresar comportamiento
    existente (solo aûÝade), lo que acota el riesgo de huella a "resuelve residuales" (verificar que
    ninguna fixture los fijaba como residual ã aquûÙ NONE).

    `solve(b^x=c)` sin simplificar)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (Fase 1, P3 ã pulido/presentaciû°n; reusa maquinaria existente)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (presentaciû°n de solve/log)
  - secondary_dimension: reuse_value (reusa `eval_log_rational` ya existente ã log_b(c) por
    factorizaciû°n prima; solo aûÝade el ruteo de la forma `Div(ln, ln)`)
  - cell: `ln(8)/ln(2) ã 3`, `ln(16)/ln(4) ã 2`, `ln(2)/ln(8) ã 1/3`, `ln(8)/ln(4) ã 3/2`;
    `solve(2^x=8) ã {3}`, `solve(4^x=8) ã {3/2}`, `solve(9^x=3) ã {1/2}`.
  - behavior_change_expected: `ln(c)/ln(b)` con c,b enteros potencias de una base comû¤n pasa de la
    forma sin simplificar al racional exacto. Snapshot `solve_two_pow_x_eq_8` actualizado de
    `{ln(8)/ln(2)}` a `{3}` (el comentario del test ya decûÙa "x = 3"). Huella guardrail+pressure NONE.
  - SOUNDNESS: `ln(c)/ln(b) = log_b(c)` es exacto; `eval_log_rational` solo devuelve Some cuando el
    log es racional (mismo soporte primo, exponentes proporcionales) y guarda `b>1` (denominador
    `ln(1)=0` declina, no divide por cero). Irracionales (`ln(7)/ln(2)`, `ln(8)/ln(3)`), no-enteros
    (`ln(x)/ln(2)`) y no-`ln` quedan EXACTAMENTE como estûÀn. Sin variable ã sin gate de dominio.
- observed:
  - el motor YA computaba `log_b(c)` racional (`eval_log_rational`, usado por `LogPowerBaseRule` para
    la forma `log(base,arg)`); solo faltaba reconocer la forma equivalente `ln(c)/ln(b)` y rutearla.
    Una regla nueva con target `DIV` (no FUNCTION) la casa.
  - la regla se registra en `logarithms/mod.rs::register` con `add_rule(Box::new(...))` ã el sistema
    de reglas NO auto-registra los `define_rule!`; hay que aûÝadirlas a la lista de `register`.
- retained learning:
  - cuando una simplificaciû°n existe para UNA grafûÙa (`log(base,arg)`) pero no para su EQUIVALENTE
    (`ln(c)/ln(b)`), el cierre suele ser una regla fina que reconoce la segunda forma y reusa el
    helper de la primera ã barato y sound. El `solve` se beneficia gratis (su salida pasa por el
    simplificador).
  - el target del rule importa: una regla sobre `Div(ln,ln)` necesita `TargetKindSet::DIV`, no
    FUNCTION; y `define_rule!` requiere registro explûÙcito en la `register` del mû°dulo.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): el cambio de base con argumentos no-enteros pero racionales-potencia
    (`ln(ã8)/ln(2)`), y `log_b(c)` simbû°lico cuando c=b^k con k simbû°lico.

    resultado final a forma canû°nica en la mûÀquina exacta de polinomios del dominio, NO confiar
    en que el simplificador global lo combine (no lo hace para estas formas) ni expandir a ciegas
    (regresarûÙa las formas factorizadas bonitas de los casos de tûˋrmino û¤nico).
  - reusar `Polynomial::from_expr`/`to_expr` da colecciû°n exacta y barata sin tocar el
    simplificador (cero blast radius en otras familias; huella NONE lo confirma).
  - residual (peldaûÝo): `end` no-variable (`sum(..., k, 1, 2n)`) y `start` simbû°lico
    (`sum(..., k, m, n)`) quedan sin colectar (conservador, correcto); la forma factorizada
    ideal `n(n+1)(n+2)/3` para cû¤bicas multi-tûˋrmino requerirûÙa un factorizador post-colecciû°n.

## 2026-06-22 - Generalizar la colecciû°n de sumatorios a cotas escaladas/afines (öÈ_{1}^{2n}(2k+1)=4nôý+4n)
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/summation_support.rs` (`try_build_polynomial_sum`: gate de colecciû°n
    por nû¤mero de variables libres en `combined`, no por forma de `end`)
- status:
  - `retained` (capability/educativo ã P3; extensiû°n directa del ciclo previo [[colecciû°n de
    sumatorios]], misma maquinaria, blast radius cero)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (Fase 1, P3 ã presentaciû°n; reusa `Polynomial` + `collect_variables`)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (forma cerrada legible con cotas no triviales)
  - secondary_dimension: reuse_value (generaliza el gate del ciclo anterior; menos cû°digo, mûÀs casos)
  - cell: `sum(2k+1, k, 1, 2n) ã 4nôý+4n`; `sum(2k+1, k, 1, n+1) ã nôý+4n+3`;
    `sum(4k-2, k, 0, n) ã 2nôýã2`; `sum(k+1, k, 1, 3n) ã 9/2ôñnôý+9/2ôñn`. Intactos:
    `sum(2k+1,1,n) ã nôý+2n`, `sum(kôý) ã 1/6ôñn(n+1)(2n+1)` factorizada,
    `sum(2k+1, k, m, n)` simbû°lico SIN colectar.
  - behavior_change_expected: los sumatorios multi-tûˋrmino con cota superior ESCALADA/AFûN
    (`2n`, `n+1`, `3n`) o inicio constante ã 1 (`0`) ahora colectan; antes solo con `end` variable
    desnuda. Huella guardrail+pressure NONE.
  - SOUNDNESS: el gate pasa de "`end` es Variable y `start` racional" a "`combined` tiene
    EXACTAMENTE una variable libre" (`cas_ast::traversal::collect_variables`) ã mûÀs general y mûÀs
    simple: una cota construida de una sola variable con `start` constante deja `combined`
    univariado; un `start` simbû°lico deja dos variables y se salta (correcto, no se puede colectar
    univariadamente). Colecciû°n exacta (`Polynomial`, BigRational); valor invariante (test por
    fuerza bruta).
- observed:
  - el gate del ciclo previo era innecesariamente estrecho (exigûÙa `end` Variable desnuda); la
    condiciû°n REAL para colectar univariadamente es "una sola variable libre en el resultado", que
    `collect_variables` decide directamente y subsume el caso desnudo.
- retained learning:
  - cuando un gate restringe por la FORMA de un input (`end` es Variable) cuando la condiciû°n real
    es una PROPIEDAD del resultado (univariado), gatear por la propiedad del resultado es mûÀs
    general y mûÀs simple a la vez ã y suele eliminar cû°digo de casos.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): cotas con DOS variables genuinas (`sum(..., k, m, n)`) siguen sin colectar
    (no son univariadas); la forma factorizada ideal para cû¤bicas multi-tûˋrmino sigue pendiente de
    un factorizador post-colecciû°n.

## 2026-06-22 - RECHAZADO: cancelar gcd num/den con numerador univariado (perturba la verificaciû°n del backend)
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/fraction_multivar_gcd.rs` (`try_multivar_gcd`: gate
    `p_num.vars.len() <= 1` ã recuento de variables COMBINADO num+den)
- status:
  - `rejected` (revertido; el ûÀrbol queda en el commit del ciclo previo)
- capture:
  - investment_class: soundness/quality (reducciû°n de factor comû¤n en resultados racionales)
  - hypothesis: gatear por "ãË2 variables en num+den" (en vez de "numerador multivariado") deja
    pasar `2y/(4x) ã y/(2x)`, `yôý/(yô°+yxôý) ã y/(yôý+xôý)`, `diff(arctan(x/y)) ã y/(xôý+yôý)` reusando
    la maquinaria de content-gcd/mono-gcd ya presente, sin tocar la ruta univariada.
  - reject_if: cualquier contador estructural de las lanes cambia sin justificaciû°n de mejora.
- observed:
  - el cambio FUNCIONA para el objetivo: `2y/(4x) ã y/(2x)`, `2y/(2x+2z) ã y/(x+z)`,
    `yôý/(yô°+yxôý) ã y/(yôý+xôý)`, `diff(arctan(x/y)) ã y/(xôý+yôý)`; `cargo test --workspace` 12272/0
    (CERO tests rotos); la ruta univariada (`(xôýã1)/(xã1) ã x+1`) intacta.
  - PERO la lane `calculus_integrate_command_matrix_smoke` baja 313ã312: el caso
    `inverse_trig_symbolic_affine_expanded_square_radius_positive_quadratic_table` pasa de
    `required=('a ã  0',)` a `('a ã  0', 'aôýxôý+2abx+bôý+4 ã  0')`. La condiciû°n extra es VACUA
    (`(ax+b)ôý+4 > 0` siempre, bajo `aã 0`): la antiderivada `arctan((ax+b)/2)/(2a)` NO cambia y
    es correcta, pero al reducir una fracciû°n INTERMEDIA de la verificaciû°n del backend (derivar
    la antiderivada y normalizar), el extractor de condiciones del backend emite el denominador
    cuadrûÀtico positivo-definido como `ã  0` en vez de reconocerlo como nunca-cero.
- decision:
  - RECHAZAR: aûÝadir una condiciû°n de dominio vacua es una regresiû°n de calidad de narraciû°n
    (el contrato de la lane fijaba la limpia `aã 0`); aceptarla actualizando el fixture serûÙa
    aceptar ruido. El fix correcto vive en el backend (que el extractor de condiciones de la
    verificaciû°n pruebe positividad-definida `(lineal)ôý+positivo > 0` y suprima el `ã 0`), no en
    el cancelador de fracciones. Es trabajo de backend separado, no parte de este ciclo.
- retained learning:
  - una mejora de simplificaciû°n de bajo blast-radius en los TESTS unitarios puede aun asûÙ
    perturbar contadores de las lanes a travûˋs de una ruta INDIRECTA: la verificaciû°n
    algebraica del backend normaliza fracciones intermedias, asûÙ que un cancelador mûÀs agresivo
    cambia quûˋ condiciones de dominio EMITE el verificador. Validar una mejora del simplificador
    SIEMPRE contra `make engine-scorecard`, no solo `cargo test`.
  - PELDAûO de alto valor: la cancelaciû°n de factor comû¤n (numûˋrico y de potencia) para
    numeradores univariados sobre denominadores con otra variable o gcd de contenido ã
    `2y/(4x)`, `yôý/(yô°+yxôý)`, `diff(arctan(x/y))`ã estûÀ a UN gate de distancia
    (`try_multivar_gcd` ya calcula content_gcd y mono_gcd; solo hay que extender el gate al
    recuento combinado de variables). Requiere PRIMERO que el extractor de condiciones de la
    verificaciû°n del backend reconozca `(lineal)ôý+positivo ã  0` como vacuo (reusar
    `is_strictly_positive_univariate_quadratic` de `prove_sign.rs`). Hacer ese sub-paso del
    backend habilita el cancelador sin la regresiû°n de condiciû°n vacua.

## 2026-06-22 - Aceptar las grafûÙas arc* de las inversas hiperbû°licas (arcsinh = asinh)
- area:
  - `crates/cas_ast/src/builtin.rs` (`BuiltinFn::from_name`: aliases `arcsinh`/`arccosh`/`arctanh`)
- status:
  - `retained` (capability/usabilidad ã P1/P3; consistencia de entrada, reusa funciones existentes)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (Fase 1; las funciones `asinh`/`acosh`/`atanh` ya existen)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (consistencia de la superficie de entrada)
  - secondary_dimension: reuse_value (alias puro a variantes existentes; cero lû°gica nueva)
  - cell: `diff(arcsinh(x)) ã 1/ã(xôý+1)`, `diff(arccosh(x)) ã 1/(ã(xã1)ã(x+1))`,
    `diff(arctanh(x)) ã 1/(1ãxôý)`, `arcsinh(0) ã 0`, `arcsinh(x) ã asinh(x)` (canoniza a la
    grafûÙa `a*`). Antes: `funciû°n [arcsinh] no definida`.
  - behavior_change_expected: `arcsinh`/`arccosh`/`arctanh` pasan de error de parseo a alias de
    `asinh`/`acosh`/`atanh`. Puramente ADITIVO (grafûÙas nuevas aceptadas); ningû¤n input previo
    cambia. Huella guardrail+pressure NONE (ningû¤n fixture usaba `arc*` hiperbû°lico ã erraba).
  - SOUNDNESS: alias sintûÀctico de entrada; sin lû°gica de dominio/valor (no aplica el guardrail
    `ValueDomain`). `from_name` es la ûNICA fuente de verdad de nombres de funciû°n (el parser la
    usa; no hay lista separada), confirmado porque el alias bastû°.
- observed:
  - asimetrûÙa: el trig YA aceptaba `arcsin` (variante distinta `Arcsin`) ademûÀs de `asin`; el
    motor EMITE `asinh`/`atanh` desde integraciû°n (`ã¨1/ã(xôý+1)=asinh(x)`, `ã¨1/(1ãxôý)=atanh(x)`)
    pero NO reconocûÙa la entrada `arcsinh`/`arctanh` ã un usuario que escribe `arcsinh` por
    analogûÙa con `arcsin` recibûÙa "no definida".
  - opciû°n elegida: alias a las variantes `Asinh`/`Acosh`/`Atanh` existentes (NO nuevas variantes
    `Arcsinh` como en el trig) ã minimal, cero blast-radius en los match exhaustivos de BuiltinFn,
    el output canoniza a `a*`. Mirror exacto del trig (variantes separadas) serûÙa L (ramas nuevas
    en derivada/formatter/latex/evaluador) sin ganancia funcional.
- retained learning:
  - cuando el motor EMITE una funciû°n pero no acepta una grafûÙa-alias de ENTRADA comû¤n para ella,
    el cierre suele ser una lûÙnea en la tabla de nombres del parser ã alias a la variante
    existente, no una variante nueva. Verifica primero que la tabla de nombres es la û¤nica fuente
    de verdad (lo es aquûÙ) antes de buscar listas paralelas.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): `arsinh`/`artanh` (prefijo "ar" tûˋcnicamente correcto para inversas
    hiperbû°licas) y las inversas hiperbû°licas sin funciû°n base (`asech`/`acsch`/`acoth`, que el
    motor no implementa) quedan fuera.

    (potencia estilo Python) es un cambio de lexer, no de tabla de nombres.

## 2026-06-22 - Evaluar la raûÙz cû¤bica de un cubo perfecto (cbrt(8) = 2)
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/difference_of_cubes_support.rs` (`rational_cbrt`,
    `try_rewrite_cbrt_perfect_cube_expr`)
  - `crates/cas_engine/src/rules/functions.rs` (`EvaluateCbrtPerfectCubeRule`, registrada)
- status:
  - `retained` (capability/consistencia ã P1/P2; `cbrt` quedaba inerte mientras `root(_,3)` y
    `sqrt` Sû evaluaban)
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (Fase 1, real-elemental; reusa el patrû°n de `sqrt` de cubo perfecto)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (consistencia de la familia de raûÙces)
  - secondary_dimension: reuse_value (espeja `rational_sqrt`/`try_rewrite_sqrt_perfect_square`)
  - cell: `cbrt(8) ã 2`, `cbrt(27) ã 3`, `cbrt(-8) ã -2`, `cbrt(64) ã 4`, `cbrt(1000) ã 10`,
    `cbrt(8/27) ã 2/3`, `cbrt(-27/8) ã -3/2`, `cbrt(1) ã 1`, `cbrt(0) ã 0`. Simbû°licos intactos:
    `cbrt(2)`, `cbrt(9)`, `cbrt(16)` (cubo PARCIAL), `cbrt(x)`, `cbrt(x^3)`; `diff(cbrt(x)) = ãx^(ã2/3)`.
  - behavior_change_expected: `cbrt(n)` con `n` cubo perfecto racional pasa de inerte a su valor
    exacto. Solo cubos PERFECTOS colapsan (display preservado para no-cubos). Huella
    guardrail+pressure NONE (ningû¤n fixture fijaba `cbrt(cubo)` sin evaluar).
  - SOUNDNESS: EXACTO ã `rational_cbrt` verifica re-cubicando los candidatos (`numer`/`denom`
    deben ser cubos perfectos), nunca f64; el signo real se conserva (`cbrt(-8) = -2`, raûÙz impar
    real). `as_rational_const` gatea a argumentos numûˋricos; no-cubos y argumentos simbû°licos
    declinan (None ã conserva). Sin lû°gica de dominio dependiente de valor (raûÙz cû¤bica real
    definida en todo ã) ã no aplica el gate `ValueDomain`.
- observed:
  - asimetrûÙa: `root(8,3) ã 2`, `(-8)^(1/3) ã -2`, `sqrt(9) ã 3` evaluaban (vûÙa
    `extract_root_factor` de `power_eval_support` y `rational_sqrt`), pero `cbrt` NO tenûÙa regla de
    evaluaciû°n ã `cbrt(8)` quedaba inerte. La funciû°n dedicada existûÙa solo para display/derivada.
  - se reusû° el patrû°n EXACTO de `sqrt` (`rational_sqrt` + `try_rewrite_sqrt_perfect_square_expr` +
    rule en functions.rs) en vez de canonizar `cbrt(x) ã x^(1/3)` (que cambiarûÙa el display de
    `cbrt(2)` a `2^(1/3)` y moverûÙa muchos fixtures).
  - `BigInt` tiene `.cbrt()` inherente (num-bigint 0.4); no hace falta `num_integer::Roots`.
- retained learning:
  - cuando una familia de funciones (raûÙces) tiene un miembro que EVALûA (`sqrt`, `root`) y otro
    que no (`cbrt`), el cierre es espejar la regla de evaluaciû°n del miembro que funciona, no
    canonizar a una forma comû¤n (que regresa el display de los casos irracionales). El gate
    "solo colapsa si es valor exacto/perfecto" preserva el display simbû°lico.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): cubo PARCIAL (`cbrt(16) ã 2ôñcbrt(2)`, anûÀlogo a `sqrt(8) ã 2ôñsqrt(2)`) y
    `cbrt(x^3) ã x` (simbû°lico, depende del signo: `cbrt(x^3)=x` real siempre, a diferencia de
    `sqrt(x^2)=|x|`) quedan fuera de este ciclo.

    `log2(2^x) ã x` (composiciû°n inversa, bonus); simbû°licos intactos `log2(3)`, `log2(6)`,
    `log2(x)`; `diff(log2(x)) = 1/(xôñln2)`; `log10`/`log(b,c)`/`ln` sin cambios.

    `log10_base_sentinel`, porque no puede asignar un nodo numûˋrico); `Log2` simplemente FALTABA,
    asûÙ que `try_rewrite_evaluate_log_expr` (y las otras 4 reglas de log que comparten el extractor)
    nunca veûÙan base 2 ã `log2(8)` quedaba inerte aunque `log10(1000)=3` y `log(2,8)=3` Sû.
  - elegido: aûÝadir el arm `Log2 ã (2,arg)` en la versiû°n MUTANTE `extract_log_base_argument` (que
    Sû puede asignar `ctx.num(2)`), evitando un `log2_base_sentinel` que rippleaba por todos los
    callers del view que hacen `base == log10_base_sentinel()`. Las 5 reglas de log ganan log2
    consistentemente (de ahûÙ el bonus `log2(2^x)=x`); huella NONE confirma cero regresiû°n.
- retained learning:
  - misma lecciû°n que [[cbrt-de-cubo-perfecto]]: un miembro de una familia (log10) evalû¤a y otro
    (log2) no porque el EXTRACTOR de base no lo reconoce. El cierre es un arm en el extractor, no
    nueva lû°gica de evaluaciû°n ã reusa toda la cadena. Preferir el punto mutante para materializar
    la base numûˋrica en vez de un sentinel que obliga a tocar todos los consumidores del view.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): `log2(1/4) ã -2` y `log10(1/100) ã -2` (argumento RACIONAL no-entero)
    siguen inertes ã `try_rewrite_evaluate_log_expr` gatea `n.is_integer()` y `eval_log_rational`
    toma enteros; falta `log_b(p/q) = log_b(p) ã log_b(q)` para potencias-de-primos-de-b racionales.

    al valor exacto por simetrûÙa (`arcsin`/`arctan` impares ã `-f(x)`; `arccos(-x) = ü - arccos(x)`),
    re-evaluando el positivo con la tabla. Huella guardrail+pressure NONE; workspace verde.

    (`arcsin`/`arccos`/`arctan`) para no tocar la trig directa (un literal negativo en `sin` no tiene
    valor de ûÀngulo notable). `canonical_table_fn_name` normaliza `asinãarcsin` etc.
- observed:
  - `rewrite_negative_trig_argument` solo manejaba la PARIDAD de trig DIRECTA (`sin(-x)`,`cos`,`tan`);
    la trig INVERSA caûÙa a `_ => None`. AdemûÀs `extract_negated_inner` reconoce `Neg(...)`/`(-1)ôñx`
    pero NO un literal `Number(-1/2)` (que es como llega `arccos(-1/2)` ya simplificado) ã por eso
    `arccos(-ã2/2)` (envuelto) funcionaba pero `arccos(-1/2)` (literal) no.
  - el test unitario inicial usû° `parse("-1/2")` (ûÀrbol `Div(-1,2)` SIN plegar) y fallû°; la regla ve
    el `Number(-1/2)` ya plegado por el simplificador ã construir el literal directo lo arregla.
- retained learning:
  - una funciû°n de "reescribir argumento negativo" puede cubrir una familia (trig directa) y olvidar
    la hermana (trig inversa); y la detecciû°n de "negativo" debe incluir el literal `Number(-n)` ya
    plegado, no solo el envoltorio `Neg`. Probar reglas que ven formas SIMPLIFICADAS con el nodo
    plegado, no con el `parse` crudo.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): argumentos surd negativos con coeficiente (`arcsin(-ã3/2)` que llega como
    `-3/2ôñ3^(-1/2)`, coef ã  -1) siguen inertes ã `extract_negated_inner` solo pela coef -1; y los
    racionales no-notables (`arccos(-1/3)`) correctamente no evalû¤an.

- area:

    ã `add_terms_signed`)
- status:

    hunt adversarial)
- capture:
  - investment_class: soundness (corrige valor errû°neo; exenta de la restricciû°n de fase)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_soundness (gcd de polinomios correcto)
  - secondary_dimension: reuse_value (espeja la rama `Sub` ya correcta, que usa `expr_key_neg`)
  - cell: `gcd(xôý+x, xôý-x) ã x` (antes `xôý+x`, ôÀno divide a xôý-x!); `gcd(xôý+x+1, xôý-x+1) ã 1`
    (coprimos; antes `xôý+x+1`); `gcd(xô°+x, xô°-x) ã x`; `gcd(xãÇ+xôý, xãÇ-xôý) ã xôý`. Intactos:
    `gcd(xôý-1, xôý-2x+1) ã x-1`, `gcd(x*(x+1), x*(x-1)) ã x`, `gcd(6,9) ã 3`.
  - behavior_change_expected: pares de polinomios que difieren SOLO en el signo de un tûˋrmino
    (`xôý+x` vs `xôý-x`) dejan de colisionar en la clave AC; el gcd estructural ya no toma el primer
    argumento entero como factor comû¤n espurio. Brute-force 16/16 vs sympy; huella NONE; workspace
    12281/0.
  - SOUNDNESS: la clave AC de una suma DEBE ser consciente del signo. `add_terms_no_sign` aplanaba
    `Add`/`Sub`/`Neg` DESCARTANDO el signo, asûÙ que `xôý+x` y `xôý-x` hasheaban a `{xôý, x}` ã
    `expr_equal_ac(xôý+x, xôý-x)=true` ã el factor comû¤n "encontrado" era el primer argumento entero
    (un no-divisor). `add_terms_signed` rastrea el signo por anidaciû°n y hashea los tûˋrminos
    negados vûÙa `expr_key_neg` (igual que la rama `Sub` ya hacûÙa). El bug solo mordûÙa cuando los
    tûˋrminos sin-signo COINCIDûAN (`+x` vs `-x`); `gcd(xôý+2x, xôý-3x) ã x` ya era correcto (coefs
    distintos).
- observed:
  - el gcd `eval` usa modo Structural (no Exact) por defecto (`select_poly_gcd_mode`); el
    estructural compara claves AC de factores `Mul`. Para entradas EXPANDIDAS (Add), el û¤nico
    "factor" es el polinomio entero; la clave AC sin-signo lo hacûÙa colisionar con su gemelo de
    signo opuesto, cortocircuitando el fallback euclidiano que Sû daba la respuesta correcta.
  - la rama `Sub` de `expr_key_hash` ya era correcta (`expr_key_neg`); solo la rama `Add`
    (vûÙa el colector sin-signo) tenûÙa el defecto ã asimetrûÙa entre dos ramas del mismo match.
- retained learning:
  - una CLAVE de igualdad/hash AC para sumas debe preservar el signo de cada tûˋrmino; un colector
    "sin signo" colisiona `A+B` con `AãB` y convierte cualquier consumidor (gcd estructural,
    cancelaciû°n de fracciones) en un wrong-answer latente cuando los tûˋrminos coinciden en
    magnitud. Cuando dos ramas de un match construyen la "misma" clave (`Add` y `Sub` aquûÙ), deben
    ser consistentes en el tratamiento del signo.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): los grupos B (inecuaciû°n de grado 4 expandida ã ã) y D (lûÙmite ããã ã 0)
    del mismo hunt siguen abiertos.

    la ruta product-sign (`should_split_product_zero_inequality`) solo se alcanza desde el pipeline
    de aislamiento MULTIPLICATIVO, asûÙ que un `Mul` factorizado funciona pero un `Add`/`Sub`
    expandido no. El equation-solver ya halla todas las raûÙces; solo faltaba puentear vûÙa factor.
  - el guard se insertû° en el CIERRE de la estrategia Isolation (û¤ltimo en el orden, tras Quadratic/
    RationalRoots/CollectTerms), donde `context_mut` y `solve_equation`/`isolate_equation` estûÀn
    disponibles ã evitando un nuevo `SolveStrategyKind` (que rosca un closure por todo el dispatch).
- retained learning:
  - cuando una capacidad existe para una GRAFûA (producto factorizado) pero no para su EQUIVALENTE
    (polinomio expandido), el puente barato es normalizar la entrada (factorizar) hacia la grafûÙa
    que ya funciona, en vez de duplicar la lû°gica. El anti-loop clave: gatear sobre la forma CRUDA
    (no la expandida) y re-entrar por el kernel de mûÀs bajo nivel (`isolate_equation`) que no
    re-expande.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): cû¤bicas/quûÙnticas IRREDUCIBLES sobre ã con raûÙz irracional (`x^3+x+1<0`)
    siguen dando aislamiento garbled ã el factorizador no las parte; necesitan aislamiento real de
    raûÙces de gradoãË3 (o punt honesto). El factorizador tampoco es completo sobre ã (deja factores
    reducibles sin raûÙz racional en algunos grados altos).

## 2026-06-22 - Logaritmos de base fraccionaria (log(1/2,16) = -4)
- area:
  - `crates/cas_math/src/logarithm_inverse_support.rs` (`signed_prime_exponent_map`,
    `eval_log_rational_full`; rama de base no-entera en `try_rewrite_evaluate_log_expr`)
- status:
  - `retained` (capability ã gradû¤a el peldaûÝo de base fraccionaria de [[log2-de-potencia-de-2]] y
    el ciclo log(b,0))
- capture:
  - investment_class: capability (Fase 1; generaliza `eval_log_rational` a base/arg racionales)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_completeness (cambio de base completo sobre ã)
  - secondary_dimension: reuse_value (reusa `prime_exponent_map`; misma lû°gica de ratio de exponentes)
  - cell: `log(1/2,16) ã -4`, `log(1/2,1/8) ã 3`, `log(1/3,9) ã -2`, `log(1/4,1/2) ã 1/2`,
    `log(2/3,9/4) ã -2`, `log(4/9,8/27) ã 3/2`. Simbû°licos: `log(1/2,6)`, `log(2/3,5)`. Intactos
    todos los de base entera (`log(4,8) ã 3/2`, `log2(8) ã 3`, `log2(1/4) ã -2`, `log(b,0)` por signo).
  - behavior_change_expected: `log_{p/q}(c)` con base racional positiva ã 1 y `c` racional positivo,
    ambos potencias-de-primos-comunes con ratio de exponentes CONSISTENTE, pasa de inerte al racional
    exacto. Huella NONE; workspace 12285/0.
  - SOUNDNESS: `eval_log_rational_full` usa el VECTOR DE EXPONENTES PRIMOS CON SIGNO de un racional
    (`r=num/den` ã exp(p) = exp_num(p) ã exp_den(p)); `log_b(c)=e` exige `eôñexp_b(p) = exp_c(p)`
    para todo primo, i.e. ratio constante y soportes iguales. Exacto (BigRational/BigInt), nunca
    f64. Dominio gateado: base>0, ã 1, arg>0. La rama de base ENTERA (ciclos 9/11) queda intacta;
    solo se aûÝade la rama no-entera.
- observed:
  - el cambio de base ya funcionaba para base ENTERA (cycle 9/11/12); el `b.is_integer()` bloqueaba
    las bases fraccionarias. La generalizaciû°n a exponentes-primos-con-signo subsume ambos
    (base/arg racionales) ã pero se mantuvo la ruta entera existente para cero regresiû°n.
- retained learning:
  - el logaritmo racional `log_b(c)` sobre ã es exactamente "ratio constante de los vectores de
    exponentes primos con signo de c y b"; extender de enteros a racionales es restar los
    exponentes de numerador y denominador. Mismo nû¤cleo, dominio mûÀs amplio.
  - residual (peldaûÝo): `log_2(ã8)` (arg con raûÙz, exponente fraccionario en el vector primo) y
    bases/args con factores transcendentes quedan fuera (sin soporte primo racional).

## 2026-06-22 - P0 soundness: log de base degenerada (log(1,1) = 0 ã undefined)
- area:

- capture:
  - investment_class: soundness (corrige valor errû°neo; exenta de la restricciû°n de fase)
  - primary_dimension: north_star_soundness (dominio del logaritmo)
  - secondary_dimension: reuse_value (un guard temprano; reusa el flujo de evaluaciû°n de log)
  - cell: `log(1,1) ã undefined` (antes `0`, FALSO: `log_1` no tiene inversa, `1^y=1 ãy`);
    `log(1,5)`, `log(0,5)`, `log(-2,4) ã undefined` (antes simbû°licos). Intactos: `log(2,8)ã3`,
    `log(1/2,16)ã-4`, `log(2,1)ã0`, `log(1/2,1)ã0`, `log(1/2,0)ãã`, `ln(eôý)ã2`.
  - behavior_change_expected: `log(b,ôñ)` con base numûˋrica `bãÊ0` o `b==1` pasa a `undefined` para
    CUALQUIER argumento (antes `log(1,1)=0` por el atajo `log(b,1)=0`, y `log(1,5)`/`log(0,ôñ)`
    simbû°licos). Bases vûÀlidas (incl. `0<b<1`) intactas. Huella NONE; workspace 12286/0.
  - SOUNDNESS: `log_b` estûÀ definido solo para `b>0, bã 1`. La base `1` es degenerada (`1^y=1` ãy,
    sin inversa); `bãÊ0` no tiene log real. El guard se chequea ANTES de las reglas por-argumento
    (que devolvûÙan `log(1,1)=0`). Solo afecta a base numûˋrica EXPLûCITA; `e`/`10`/`2` de
    `ln`/`log10`/`log2` son vûÀlidas. Una base en `(0,1)` NO es degenerada (`log_{1/2}` vûÀlido).
- observed:

    rama `n.is_one() ã 0` no miraba la base. Un guard de validez de base al inicio lo cierra y
    ademûÀs vuelve `undefined` (mûÀs correcto que simbû°lico) los `log(0,ôñ)`/`log(neg,ôñ)`.
- retained learning:
  - las reglas de "valor en un punto especial del ARGUMENTO" (`log(b,1)=0`, `log(b,0)=ôÝã`) deben
    estar gateadas por la validez de la BASE; una base degenerada invalida el logaritmo ENTERO,
    asûÙ que el chequeo de base va PRIMERO. Mismo principio que el fix de signo de `log(b,0)`.
